baseline.b4b5_route: two graph nodes owned_flash_tile_gqa -> owned_flash_combine_hd; default-off; W==D saturates +5.66-5.85%@4096 < +7% gate

fold_combine_one_kernel_no_grid_sync.mechanism: the tile splits KV across S workgroups (grid.x=Hkv=8, grid.y=S; @ctx1024 S=48 -> 384 workgroups) each writing a per-split partial PV + (m,l) meta to GLOBAL. Combining them is a CROSS-WORKGROUP log-sum-exp reduction. Cross-workgroup reduction in one kernel needs grid-wide sync (cooperative groups / hipLaunchCooperativeKernel) or a fragile atomics+spin software barrier. tinygrad's HCQ launch path has NO cooperative-launch support (grep: 'cooperative' appears ONLY in autogen bindings hip.py/hsa.py, NOT in runtime/support/hcq.py, ops_amd.py, graph/hcq.py, or engine/realize.py). So a true folded-combine single kernel is impossible on this runtime.

remaining_single_node_shape.answer: no-split, one workgroup per (kv)head over full KV
remaining_single_node_shape.parallelism: Hkv=8 workgroups (gqa-packed, 4 q-heads/wg) or Hq=32 (no pack) -- vs the split form's 384 @ctx1024. 8-32 workgroups on 96 CUs = 8-33% CU occupancy, reverting to exactly the batch-1 under-occupancy that split-KV was built to fix. Each wg does a full-KV serial loop -> latency unhidden. PARALLELISM_INSUFFICIENT.

persistent_cooperative_grid.answer: UNSUPPORTED on tinygrad HCQ (no cooperative launch)

preserves_gqa_v_reuse_coalesced.answer: only the split gqa-packed tile does; no-split per-q-head loses GQA V-reuse (4x redundant V reads)

can_serve_as_opaque_kv_read.answer: YES -- but the EXISTING two-node tile already does (see redirect)

extra_finding: llama itself uses TWO kernels (flash_attn_tile + flash_attn_combine_results<128>), NOT a folded single tile (llama-flash-attn-tile-oracle:18). So eliminating the combine is NOT the lever -- llama's 5x advantage is TILE CODEGEN QUALITY (LDS ds_load_b128 + v_dot2 + online softmax/PV), which is the UNBOUNDED native-codegen lane (deferred). The 'single fused tile' premise is refuted on two independent grounds: (1) infeasible without grid sync, (2) not what makes llama fast.
redirect: the EXISTING two-node owned tile (amdgcn_flash_decode, model.py:990) ALREADY reads cache K/V natively via custom_kernel ([Hkv,MAXC,Hd]); it is ALREADY the opaque attention read needed to remove the KV copy. Feed it the persistent cache (post opaque-append) instead of assigned_kv -> the runtime-KV follow-on captures the MEASURED +1.4ms/+8tok/s, with no new tile.
